sections.header: Verses 1–7 — “Keep Me Safe from the Hands of the Violent”

heb.paragraph: "Rescue me, LORD, from evildoers; protect me from the violent (ḥāmās)" (v.1). A David lament. The enemies devise evil (v.2), sharpen tongues like serpents, have cobra venom on their lips (v.3). "Keep me safe from the hands of the violent, who devise ways to trip my feet" (v.4). Hidden snares, ropes, nets along the path (v.5). David’s confession: "You are my God" (v.6). "Sovereign LORD, my strong deliverer, you shield my head in the day of battle" (v.7).

notes.note: "Rescue me from evildoers. They sharpen their tongues like serpents; the poison of vipers is on their lips." The serpent imagery: forked tongues, venom. Speech as poison.
"I say to the LORD, ‘You are my God.’ Sovereign LORD, my strong deliverer, you shield my head in the day of battle." The confession and the confidence: you are my God; you shield my head.

refs.note: The tongue is a restless evil, full of deadly poison—matching the snake imagery.
Paul quotes verse 3: 'The poison of vipers is on their lips.'
The malicious hide hatred with lying lips, but evil will be exposed.

hist.context: Psalm 140 is an individual lament against violent enemies. David faces those who plan evil, stir up war daily, and sharpen their tongues like serpents. The imagery is both physical (violence, traps) and verbal (slander, lies). This psalm was paired with evening prayer in Jewish liturgy, committing the night's dangers to God's protection.

sections.header: Verses 8–13 — “I Know That the LORD Secures Justice for the Poor”

heb.paragraph: "I know that the LORD secures justice (mishpāṭ) for the poor and upholds the cause of the needy" (v.12). David asks God to frustrate the wicked’s desires (v.8). May burning coals fall on them (v.10). The slanderer will not be established (v.11). The psalm’s theological declaration: "I KNOW that the LORD secures justice for the poor" (v.12). Not hope, not wish — I KNOW. "Surely the righteous will praise your name, and the upright will live in your presence" (v.13).

notes.note: "May burning coals fall on them; may they be thrown into the fire." The imprecation: fire for the fire-starters. Coals for those who heated hatred.

"I KNOW that the LORD secures justice for the poor. Surely the righteous will praise your name, and the upright will live in your presence." Confidence: justice will come. The poor will be vindicated. The righteous will dwell with God.

refs.note: Whoever digs a pit will fall into it—the psalm's prayer for reversed plots.
Haman is hanged on the gallows he built for Mordecai—evil recoiling.
The beast and false prophet are thrown into the lake of fire—ultimate judgment.

hist.context: The second half prays for divine justice: may the evil of their lips engulf them, may burning coals fall on them. The psalmist trusts that 'the LORD secures justice for the poor and upholds the cause of the needy.' The concluding confidence—'the upright will live in your presence'—anchors the lament in covenant hope. Even amid persecution, the righteous will dwell with God.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: " The confession and the confidence: you are my God…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVDo not grant the wicked their desires, Lord; do not let their plans succeed.ESVGrant not, O LORD, the desires of the wicked; do not further their evil plot, or they will be exalted! SelahNIVI say to the Lord, “You are my God.” Hear, Lord, my cry for mercy.ESVI say to the LORD, You are my God; give ear to the voice of my pleas for mercy, O LORD!

rec.text: ""Rescue me from evildoers. They sharpen their tongues like serpents; the poison of vipers is on their lips." The serpent imagery: forked tongues, venom. Speech as poison."

rows.label: Serpent Tongues — 140:1–7
rows.text: "Tongues like serpents." Venom on their lips. "You are my God. You shield my head."

hebtext.note: "Rescue me, LORD, from evildoers; protect me from the violent (ḥāmās)" (v.1). A David lament. The enemies devise evil (v.2), sharpen tongues like serpents, have cobra venom on their lips (v.3). "Keep me safe from the hands of the violent, who devise ways to trip my feet" (v.4). Hidden snares, ropes, nets along the path (v.5). David’s confession: "You are my God" (v.6). "Sovereign LORD, my strong deliverer, you shield my head in the day of battle" (v.7).

"I know that the LORD secures justice (mishpāṭ) for the poor and upholds the cause of the needy" (v.12). David asks God to frustrate the wicked’s desires (v.8). May burning coals fall on them (v.10). The slanderer will not be established (v.11). The psalm’s theological declaration: "I KNOW that the LORD secures justice for the poor" (v.12). Not hope, not wish — I KNOW. "Surely the righteous will praise your name, and the upright will live in your presence" (v.13).

themes.note: Justice peaks: "I KNOW the LORD secures justice for the poor." Judgment: imprecation against the violent. Faith: the confidence declaration amid threat.
